questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: In an antiform, the oldest beds (if not overturned) are found
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An anticline/antiform exposes the oldest strata in its core.

**Working.**
Core.

**Answer.** In the core

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A reverse fault forms under a stress regime in which
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Andersonian theory: reverse (thrust) faulting has horizontal $\sigma_1$, vertical $\sigma_3$.

**Working.**
Horizontal $\sigma_1$.

**Answer.** $\sigma_1$ horizontal

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A rock is loaded with $\sigma_1=120\,$MPa and $\sigma_3=40\,$MPa. The shear stress on a plane whose normal is at $30^\circ$ to $\sigma_1$ is _____ MPa.

figure.caption: σ₁=120, σ₃=40 MPa; plane at 30° to σ₁

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: On an equal-area (Schmidt) stereonet, a plane is represented as a
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A plane plots as a great circle; its pole plots as a point.

**Working.**
Great circle.

**Answer.** Great circle

figure.caption: A dipping plane as a great circle

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: Which features indicate the younging (way-up) direction in deformed strata?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Graded bedding, cross-bed truncations and ripple/desiccation features are way-up criteria; cleavage–bedding angle gives fold position, not younging.

**Working.**
Options 1, 2, 4.

**Answer.** Way-up criteria

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $30^\circ$ and dips $45^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=45^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $45^\circ$

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $90^\circ$ and dips $65^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=65^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $65^\circ$

**Working.**
